title: 1026. Maximum Difference Between Node and Ancestor
question:
Given the root of a binary tree, find the maximum value V for which there exists different nodes A and B where V = |A.val - B.val| and A is an ancestor of B.

(A node A is an ancestor of B if either: any child of A is equal to B, or any child of A is an ancestor of B.)

 

Example 1:

Input: [8,3,10,1,6,null,14,null,null,4,7,13]
Output: 7
Explanation: 
We have various ancestor-node differences, some of which are given below :
|8 - 3| = 5
|3 - 7| = 4
|8 - 1| = 7
|10 - 13| = 3
Among all possible differences, the maximum value of 7 is obtained by |8 - 1| = 7.

 

Note:

  1. The number of nodes in the tree is between 2 and 5000.
  2. Each node will have value between 0 and 100000.
code: # Definition for a binary tree node.\u000A# class TreeNode(object):\u000A#     def __init__(self, x):\u000A#         self.val \u003D x\u000A#         self.left \u003D None\u000A#         self.right \u003D None\u000A\u000Aclass Solution(object):\u000A    def maxAncestorDiff(self, root):\u000A        \u0022\u0022\u0022\u000A        :type root: TreeNode\u000A        :rtype: int\u000A        \u0022\u0022\u0022\u000A        def dfs(node, l\u003DNone, s\u003DNone):\u000A            if not node: return 0\u000A            print l, s, node.val\u000A            l \u003D max(node.val if l is None else l, node.val)\u000A            s \u003D min(node.val if s is None else s, node.val)\u000A            print l, s\u000A            return max(dfs(node.left, l, s), dfs(node.right, l, s), l \u002D s)\u000A            \u000A        \u000A        return dfs(root)
